title: OR CFP Quarterly Trend Sheet - Q1 2025
content:
In Q1 2025, Oregonâ€™s Clean Fuels Program (CFP) saw a40.34% year-on-year decline in total credits. A combination of lower volumes in RD and BD anda lower CI
benchmark for Gasoline could be the potential reason for this fall. Deficits increased by 23.24% YoY, totaling 816,792. Diesel deficits grew by 31.45% to 231,204, largely due to higher volumes, despite the rise in the CI benchmark. Gasoline-related deficits saw an 18.62% increase, reaching 473,671, possibly influenced by a decrease in the benchmark CI.
author: Aru Somya
